ResourceHeader.Description: Guam serves as a critical strategic node for NASA’s satellite communication and scientific research in the Pacific. The island hosts key NASA infrastructure—such as the Guam Remote Ground Terminal—and educational consortiums focused on aerospace, earth science, and STEM outreach.
ResourceHeader.Acknowledgement: As defined in Rasson J.L., (2001). The status of world-wide network of magnetic observatories, their location and instrumentation, Contributions to Geophysics & Geodesy, 31, 1, pp 427-453

InformationURL.Name: IAGA Guides and Standards
InformationURL.Description:
Overview of IAGA Guides and Standards which obtained within its
          Divisions and Working Groups. Information about observatories
          is maintained by the "Geomagntic Observatories" (V-OBS) working group.
